answer.content:
<html>
<head>
    <title>Graph (Directed)</title>
    <style>
        #graph {
            border: 1px solid black;
        }
    </style>
</head>
<body>
    <canvas id="graph" width="400" height="400"></canvas>
    <script>
        const ctx = document.getElementById('graph').getContext('2d');
        const graph = new Graph();

        graph.addNode('A', { x: 100, y: 100 });
        graph.addNode('B', { x: 150, y: 100 });
        graph.addNode('C', { x: 200, y: 100 });
        graph.addNode('D', { x: 250, y: 100 });
        graph.addNode('E', { x: 300, y: 100 });
        graph.addNode('F', { x: 350, y: 100 });

        graph.addEdge('A', 'B', { weight: 5 });
        graph.addEdge('A', 'C', { weight: 3 });
        graph.addEdge('A', 'D', { weight: 4 });
        graph.addEdge('B', 'E', { weight: 2 });
        graph.addEdge('B', 'F', { weight: 1 });
        graph.addEdge('C', 'D', { weight: 6 });
        graph.addEdge('C', 'E', { weight: 7 });
        graph.addEdge('C', 'F', { weight: 8 });
        graph.addEdge('D', 'F', { weight: 9 });

        graph.draw(ctx);
    </script>
</body>
</html>

messages.content: Generate a single HTML file with JavaScript demonstrating the concept Graph (Directed). Only respond in a single HTML file.
